content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me locate atom index 4 (C:4) in this image.

From the 3D visualization, I can see atom C:4 is located in the central-right portion of the molecule. Looking at the connections to C:4, I can identify:

- C:5 (carbon) - connected to C:4 (to the left of C:4)
- C:3 (carbon) - connected to C:4 (above and to the left)
- H:65 (hydrogen) - connected to C:4 (above and to the right)
- H:66 (hydrogen) - connected to C:4 (below and to the right)

All four bonds are clearly visible in the 3D structure. Carbon atom 4 has the typical tetrahedral geometry with four covalent bonds: two to carbon atoms (C:3 and C:5) and two to hydrogen atoms (H:65 and H:66).

Regarding the geomconf_index, from the file naming convention in the working directory, I can see the file is named "geomconf_5795.xyz", which confirms the geomconf_index is 5795.

The neighbor atoms of atom index 4 (C) are:
- Atom index 3, element C
- Atom index 5, element C  
- Atom index 65, element H
- Atom index 66, element H

Geomconf_index: 5795

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 4, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
